title: Population cages with permuted diet
steps: Studies followed https://www.protocols.io/private/90C4D66F3DAF2BEDBF93273D5ED75E1F, but the diet was permuted over the 26 generations of the study.
Generations 1-4 were fed 1:2 P:C laboratory diet, 5-20 the 1:16 P:C diet and 21-26 the 1:2 P:C diet.
Two addition population cage studies were then completed.
In the first cage study the laboratory diet was replaced with natural fruits.
In the second study the two D. melanogaster mitotypes were completed against an inbred line of D. simulans si III collected by JWOB in east Africa.

For the population cage and fruit studies, the mtDNA frequency of larvae harbouring Dahomey or Alstonville mtDNA was determined by allele-specific PCR and independently corroborated by Sanger sequencing, as above using the ND4L forward 5’-TAAACAAACTAATCTAACTAATA-3’, reverse 3’-GGTTGTGATATATTATCTTATGG-5’ and reverse 3’-TATATTAATTGGTATTTTTTCTG-5’ primer.

The fruit diet was constructed by adding 150 g of passionfruit or banana in a standard base containing 1% agar, 0.1% nipagen, 0.1% propionic acid and 0.001% phosphoric acid. The final volume was set at 300ml.
creator: Wen Aw
